scripts.description: 30 turns across 3 distinct issues; turn 3 states exact amount $123.45, turn 28 asks for it (recall test)

turns.user_message: Hi there, I'm having issues with three different orders. First, I want to check the status of order O001.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent acknowledges and calls lookup_order('O001') to retrieve status.

turns.user_message: That order looks good. Now let me ask about order O002 - I think there might be an issue with the total amount.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent calls lookup_order('O002') to check details.

turns.user_message: Yeah, I see the subtotal is $123.45 on that one. But I'm concerned about the charges. What exactly was I charged?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent confirms the $123.45 subtotal and explains total with tax and shipping.

turns.user_message: And I also need to check on another issue with my account - can you verify my email is correct?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent calls get_customer to retrieve customer info and confirm email.

turns.user_message: Email looks fine. But now I'm worried about shipping times. When should order O001 arrive?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent references already-fetched O001 delivery_date.

turns.user_message: That's sooner than expected actually. Let me ask about the third order then - O003.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent calls lookup_order('O003').

turns.user_message: Hmm, O003 shows free shipping. How did I get free shipping on that?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent checks order internal_flags and notes mentioning free shipping policy.

turns.user_message: Got it. So can you check what my current tier is?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent references customer record and states tier.

turns.user_message: I'm only silver tier. That's frustrating. What are the requirements to get to gold?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent acknowledges customer frustration and explains tier system (general knowledge, not in fixture data).

turns.user_message: Well, what's the total amount I've spent so far?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent references total_spend from customer record.

turns.user_message: That's not that much. But anyway, can you tell me more about order O002's shipping info?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent refers back to O002 data already retrieved.

turns.user_message: I see the tracking number. Let me ask - what's the difference between being in transit vs delivered?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent explains order statuses (general knowledge).

turns.user_message: Okay. Now can you tell me how many items I ordered in O001?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent counts items from O001 data (2 items).

turns.user_message: Two items. What were those exactly?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent lists item names and SKUs from O001.

turns.user_message: Got it. So I want to understand my refund options. If I wanted to return O001, how much would I get back?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent explains refund would be based on order total and policy.

turns.user_message: And what if I return O002 instead?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent explains O002 refund amount would be different.

turns.user_message: What about O003? That one was cheaper right?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent compares O003 total to previous orders.

turns.user_message: Yes that's the book purchase. Do you have my name on file?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent retrieves customer name from get_customer call.

turns.user_message: That's right. And what address do you have for me?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent would need to look up shipping_address from one of the orders or acknowledge address lookup limitation.

turns.user_message: So those are the three addresses from my orders. Have I used different addresses?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent reviews shipping addresses from O001, O002, O003.

turns.user_message: I moved recently so those are all old. But let's talk about something else - when was my account created?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent retrieves account_created timestamp from customer record.

turns.user_message: That's about right. So I've been a customer for a while. Have any of my orders been returned?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent would need to review all orders (system limitation - only lookup_order available per ID).

turns.user_message: I remember I had to return something. Let me check if you can look up O007 for me.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent calls lookup_order('O007') which has status 'RETURNED'.

turns.user_message: Yes that's the one. It was defective. Did I get my money back?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent reviews O007 which shows returned/defective status.

turns.user_message: So what's the return reason recorded?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent cites 'Defective' from O007 return_reason field.

turns.user_message: Right. Now I want to double-check one of my earlier orders again. Was it O001 or O002?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent helps clarify which order (both have been discussed).

turns.user_message: O002, yes. I need to know what the subtotal was once more.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent recalls $123.45 subtotal from turn 3 statement (context management test).

turns.user_message: Wait, can you remind me - what was the exact dollar amount on that order? I need it for my records.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent correctly recalls and states $123.45 subtotal from earlier turn 3 (tests memory/context retention across 25 turns).

turns.user_message: Perfect, got it written down. One more thing - do you have my phone number on file?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent retrieves phone from customer record (if get_customer was called) or states information not available.

turns.user_message: Great, that's all correct. Thanks for your help organizing all this!
turns.expected_outcome: Agent summarizes the conversation and closes politely.
